hadiths.ar:
1563  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ر ُّرَّإرَّة ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال هِ وَّ إ لِهِ إَّلص  ُّحَّى  
ق َّظهَّارِ إ َّرْن َّغَّ رَّكَّعَّاب ٍ ق َّال 
مِن َّ إَّلي  َّمَّان ِ إَّل َّي ِى  ن َّعْذَّ إَّلط  ُّهْزِ .
hadiths.en:
Abdul Wahid ibn al-Mukhtar al-Ansari narrated from Abu Ja'far {a.s}, who said:  
"I asked him about the Duha prayer” 
Imam {a.s} said: ‘The first people to perform it were your people, for they were among 
the heedless, so they performed it.  
The Messenger of Allah {saws} did not perform it.'  
He also said: 'Imam Ali {a.s} passed by a man who was performing it, and Imam Ali {a.s} 
said: ‘What is this prayer?’  
The man replied: ‘Should I leave it, O’ Commander of the Faithful?’  
He (Imam Ali {a.s}) said: ‘Would I forbid a servant when he prays?’". 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 566 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 117 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 
101 • Tafseer Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Part 14, p. 348 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 87, H. 1562
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٧، ح ١٥٦٢

hadiths.ar:
1564   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ عَّن ْذُّ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ُّ سِن َّان ٍ ، إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ِ إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ ق ِى  س َّهْزِ رَّمَّص َّان َّ ق َّق َّال 
رَّكْعَّه ً مِن ْهَّا إَّلْوَّي ْزُّ وَّ رَّكْعَّي َّان ِ ق َّن ْلَّ صَّلاَّة ِ إَّلْق َّح ْزِ كَّذ َّلِك َّ كَّان َّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ   وَّ إ لِهِ ي ُّصَّلِّى  وَّ لَّوْ كَّان َّ ق َّص ْلاً  
كَّان َّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّ ل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ إ َّعْمَّلَّ ن ِهِ وَّ إ َّجَّق  َّ .
hadiths.en:
Zurarah narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} that He said:  
"The Messenger of Allah {saws} never prayed the Duha prayer."  
So I (Zurarah) said to him, "Did you not inform me that He {saws} used to pray four rak'ahs 
in the forenoon?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "Yes, but he used to count it among the eight rak'ahs that are prayed 
after Dhuhr." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 566 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 116 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 
100 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 234 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 87, H. 1563
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٧، ح ١٥٦٣

hadiths.en:
Abdullah ibn Sinan asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} about the 
prayer during the month of Ramadan.  
Imam {a.s} said: "Thirteen rak'ahs, including the Witr prayer and two rak'ahs before the 
Fajr prayer. This is how the Messenger of Allah {saws} used to pray, and if there was any 
greater virtue in adding more, the Messenger of Allah {saws} would have been the most 
diligent and deserving of it."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 566 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 2, p. 137 • 
Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 3, p. 69 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 467 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 436 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, 
p. 43 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 87, H. 1564
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٧، ح ١٥٦٤

hadiths.en:
Uqbah ibn Khalid asked Him (Imam Abu Abdullah {a.s}) about a man who was 
praying and another man called out to him, causing him to become distracted and 
respond to the person's request. What should he do?  
Imam {a.s} said: "He should continue with his prayer."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 87, H. 1565
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٧، ح ١٥٦٥

hadiths.ar:
1567  -  وَّ رَّوَّى سَّمَّاعَّه ُّ ن ْن ُّ مِهْزَّإن َّ عَّن ْهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال م ُّ إَّلن  َّاسَّ إ ِذ َّإ كَّان َّ 
لَّهُّ عَّس ْزُّ سِن ِي ن َّ .
hadiths.en:
Imran Al-Halabi narrated from Imam {a.s} that He said:  
"It is recommended to lighten (shorten) the prayer because of forgetfulness."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 567 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1002 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, 
p. 236 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 87, H. 1566
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٧، ح ١٥٦٦

hadiths.ar: 1568  - وق ال إلصاذق  علي ه إلسلام: " إ ذ إ صلي ث  معهم ع ق ز لك  ن عذذ من  ج الق ك .
hadiths.en:
Sama’a bin Mehran narrated from Imam {a.s} that He said:  
"The charity given by a boy, his emancipation (freeing a slave), and leading people in 
prayer are permissible if he is ten years old."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 567 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1180 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, 
p. 322 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 87, H. 1567
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٧، ح ١٥٦٧

hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"If you pray with them (in congregation), you will be forgiven according to the number 
of those who oppose you."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 87, H. 1568
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٧، ح ١٥٦٨

hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Him (Imam Al -Sadiq {a.s}) through Abd al -Rahman ibn Abi 
Abdillah that Imam {a.s} said:  
"If you pray, then pray in your sandals (shoes) if they are clean, for that is from the 
Sunnah."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 568 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 233 • Wasail Al-Shia, 
Vol. 4, p. 424 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 426 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 87, H. 1569
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٧، ح ١٥٦٩

hadiths.ar:
1571  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ عَّان ِذ ٍ إَّلْأ َّجْمَّسِى ِّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ: ذَّج َّلْث ُّ عَّلَّى إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّ وَّ إ َّن َّا إ ُّرِي ذُّ إ َّن ْ إ َّسْا َّلَّه  عَّن ِ إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ ق َّان ْي َّذَّإ َّن ِى  
مِن ْ ع َّي ْزِ إ َّن ْ إ َّسْا َّلَّهُّ ق َّق َّال سِ إَّلْمَّق ْزُّوص َّاب ِ لَّمْ ي َّسْا َّلْك َّ عَّم َّا سِوَّى ذ َّلِك َّ .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated by Al -Halabi from Him (Imam Al -Sadiq {a.s}) that He said: "If you 
pray any of the prayers during travel outside of its (regular) time, it will not harm you."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 568 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 115  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 87, H. 1570
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٧، ح ١٥٧٠

hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Aidh al -Ahmasi: "I entered upon Imam Al -Sadiq {a.s} while I 
intended to ask him about prayer. Without me asking, Imam {a.s} began (by saying):  
'If you meet Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, with the five obligatory prayers, He will 
not ask you about anything beyond that.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 568 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 4, p. 12 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 
4, p. 69 • Ithbāt al-Hudāt, Part 4, p. 190 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 87, H. 1571
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٧، ح ١٥٧١

hadiths.ar:
1573  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّن ْذُّ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ُّ سِن َّان ٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّ ق َّالَّ: ق ُّلْث ُّ لَّهُّ إ َّج ْن ِزْن ِى  عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ مِن ْ صَّلاَّة ِ إَّلن  َّوَّإق ِلِ مَّا  
لأَّ ي َّذْرِى  مَّا هُّوَّ مِن ْ كَّي ْزَّي ِهَّا كَّي ْف َّ ي َّصْن َّغُّ ق َّال كُّون َّ ق َّذْ ق َّص َّى ن ِق َّذْرِ مَّا  
عَّلِمَّ هُّ مِن ْ ذ َّلِك َّ، عِي س َّه ٍ لأَّ ن ُّذ َّ مِن ْهَّا إ َّوْ 
جَّاج َّه ٍ لِأ َّح ٍ مُّو ْمِن ٍ ق َّلاَّ س َّى ْءَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ س ُّع ُّلُّهُّ لِح َّمْغِ إَّلذ ُّن ْي َّا وَّ إَّلي  َّ س َّاع ُّلِ ن ِهَّا عَّن ِ إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ ق َّعَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلْق َّص َّاءُّ وَّ إ ِلأ َّ لَّق ِى َّ  
إَّلل َّهَّ وَّ هُّوَّ مُّسْي َّح ِف    مُّي َّهَّاوِن   مُّص َّي ِّغ  لِحُّزْمَّه ِ رَّسُّولِ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ن  َّهُّ لأَّ ي َّق ْذِرُّ عَّلَّى إَّلْق َّص َّاءِ ق َّهَّلْ  
ي ُّح ْز ِى  إ َّن ْ ي َّ ي َّصَّذ َّق َّ ق َّسَّكَّث َّ مَّلِي  اً ي ُّم َّ ق َّال ن َّى ذ َّلِك َّ مُّذ    
لِكُّلِّ مِسْكِي ن ٍ مَّكَّان َّ كُّلِّ صَّلاَّة ٍ  ق َّال 
إَّلل َّي ْلِ مُّذ   وَّ لِكُّلِّ رَّكْعَّي َّي ْن ِ مِن ْ صَّلاَّة ِ إَّلن  َّهَّارِ مُّذ رْن َّغِ رَّكَّعَّاب ٍ مِن ْ صَّلاَّة ِ إَّلن  َّهَّارِ 
لأَّ ي َّق ْذِرُّ ق َّال لاَّة ِ إَّلل َّي ْلِ وَّ مُّذ   لِصَّلاَّة ِ إَّلن  َّهَّارِ وَّ إَّلص َّلاَّة ُّ إ َّق ْص َّلُّ وَّ إَّلص َّلاَّة ُّ إ َّق ْص َّلُّ وَّ إَّلص َّلاَّة ُّ إ َّق ْص َّلُّ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said: "A believer remains in a state of 
spiritual reward (mu'aqqib) as long as they are in a state of ritual purity (wudhu)”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 87, H. 1572
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٧، ح ١٥٧٢

hadiths.ar:
والحمد 
التجميع الإنجليزي للجزء الأول– الطبعة الأولى [2024]  
"من لا يحضره الفقيه" قداكتمل، من تأليف   
الش يخ السعيد أبو جعفر محمد بن علي بن الحسي بن موسى بن بابويه  
القمي، قدس الله روحه ونور الله قبره  
نقدم جهودنا في هذا التجميع في خدمة إمامنا الزمان، الإمام محمد بن الحسن 
المهدي؛ القائم؛ الحجة؛ المنتظر؛ الخلف الصالح؛ الموعود؛ بقية الله؛ صاحب الزمان 
وأسلافه المعصومي. والحمد لله رب العالمي،  
والصلاة والسلام على س يدنا النبي محمد وعلى ذريته الطاهرة،   
وخليفته أمير المؤمني الإمام علي بن أبي طالب  
هنا نختتم كلمتنا في هذا الجزء الأول – الطبعة الأو2024] 
والحمد 
اللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلىَ فَاطِمَةَ وَأَبِيهَا وَبَعْلِهَا وَبَنِيهَا وَالسِِِّّّ الْمُس ْتَوْدَعِ فِيهَا بِعَدَدِ مَا أَحَاطَ بِهِ عِلْمُكَ  
يََ اللهُ. يََ اللهُ. يََ اللهُ. يََ اللهُ. يََ اللهُ. يََ اللهُ. يََ اللهُ  
رَبَّنَا آتِنَا فيِ الدُّنْيَا حَس َنَةً وَفيِ الْآخِرَةِ حَس َنَةً وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ– بَِِقِّ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 87, H. 1573
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٧، ح ١٥٧٣

hadiths.chapter: 1. CHAPTER ON THE REASONS FOR THE OBLIGATION OF
hadiths.ar:
1575   -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّن َّارَّك   إَّلْعَّق َّزْق ُّوق ِى  ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ مُّوسَّى ن ْن ِ ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال عَّث ِ إَّلز  َّكَّاة ُّ ق ُّوي اً 
لِلْق ُّق َّزَّإءِ وَّ ي َّوْق ِي زإً لِأ َّمْوَّإلِهِمْ
hadiths.en:
Abdullah ibn Sinan reported from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad 
Al-Sadiq {a.s} who said:  
"Indeed, Allah {SWT}, the Almighty and Majestic, has ordained Zakat just as He has 
ordained prayer.  
If a man were to carry the Zakat and give it openly, there would be no fault upon him in 
that, because Allah {SWT}, the Almighty and Majestic, has decreed for the poor in the 
wealth of the rich that which suffices them.  
And if Allah {SWT} knew that what Allah {SWT} has ordained for them would not suffice 
them, then Allah {SWT} would have increased it.  
The deprivation faced by the poor in what they are given stems from the refusal of 
those who withhold their rightful dues, not from the insufficiency of the obligation."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 3, p. 498 • Ilal al-Sharai’, Vol. 2, p. 368 • Al-
Wafi, Vol. 10, p. 47 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 9, p. 10 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 93, p. 18 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 1, H. 1574

hadiths.chapter: 1. CHAPTER ON THE REASONS FOR THE OBLIGATION OF
hadiths.ar: 1576 - وَّ رَّوَّى مُّوسَّى ن ْن ُّ ن َّكْزٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ مُّوسَّى ن ْن ِ ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال  ن ِالز  َّكَّاة ِ
hadiths.en:
Mubarak Al -Aqrqoufi narrated from Abu Al -Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al -
Kadhim {a.s} who said:  
"Zakat was established as sustenance for the poor and a means of increasing their 
wealth."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al Mahasin, Part 2, p. 319 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 3, p. 498 • Ilal 
al-Sharai’, Vol. 2, p. 368 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 10, p. 47 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 9, p. 10 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 9, p. 
211 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 93, p. 18 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 1, H. 1575

hadiths.chapter: 1. CHAPTER ON THE REASONS FOR THE OBLIGATION OF
hadiths.ar:
1577  - وَّ رَّوَّى جَّزِي ز   عَّن ْ ر ُّرَّإرَّة َّ وَّ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ مُّسْلِمٍ : إ َّن  َّهُّمَّا ق َّالأَّ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّ إ َّ رَّإ َّي ْث َّ ق َّوْل إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ   
«۝  إ ِن  َّمَّا إَّلص َّذَّق ٰاب ُّ لِلْق ُّق َّزٰإءِ وَّ إَّلْمَّسٰاكِي ن ِ وَّ إَّلْعٰامِلِي ن َّ عَّلَّي ْهٰا وَّ إَّلْمُّو َّل َّق َّه ِ ق ُّلُّون ُّهُّمْ وَّ ق ِى  إَّلزِّ ق ٰاب ِ وَّ إَّلْع ٰارِمِي ن َّ وَّ ق ِى  سَّن ِي لِ 
إَّللّٰهِ وَّ إِن ْن ِ إَّلس َّن ِي لِ ق َّزِي ص َّه ً مِن َّ إَّللّٰهِ  ۝»  إ كُّل ُّ هَّو ُّلأَّءِ ي ُّعْطَّى وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ لأَّ ي َّعْزِف ُّ ق َّق َّال 
ج َّمِي عاً لِأ َّن  َّهُّمْ  ي ُّق ِز ُّون َّ لَّهُّ ن ِالط َّاعَّه ِ ى  مَّن ْ ي َّعْزِف ُّ  
ذُّون َّ مَّن ْ لأَّ ي َّعْزِف ُّ لَّمْ ي ُّوج َّذْ لَّهَّا مَّوْص ِغ  وَّ إ ِن  َّمَّا ي ُّعْطِى  مَّن ْ لأَّ ي َّعْزِف ُّ لِي َّزْع َّث َّ ق ِى  إَّلذِّي ن ِ  ق َّي َّي ْن ُّث َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ ق َّا َّم َّا إَّلْي َّوْمَّ ق َّلاَّ 
ي ُّعْطِهَّا إ َّن ْث َّ وَّ إ َّصْحَّان ُّك َّ إ ِلأ َّ مَّن ْ  ي َّ عْزِف ُّ ق َّمَّن ْ وَّج َّذْب َّ مِن ْ هَّو ُّلأَّءِ إَّلْمُّسْلِمِي ن َّ عَّارِق اً ق َّا َّعْطِهِ ذُّون َّ إَّلن  َّاسِ 
«  عَّام   وَّ إَّلْن َّاق ِى  ج َّاض 
إَّلل َّهُّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ وَّ لأَّ ي ُّوج َّذُّ لَّهَّا إ َّهْل لل َّهَّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ق َّزَّ ض َّ لِلْق ُّق َّزَّإءِ  
ق ِى  مَّالِ إَّلْأ َّع ْن ِي َّاءِ مَّا ي َّسَّعُّهُّمْ وَّ لَّوْ عَّلِمَّ إ َّن  َّ ذ َّلِك َّ لأَّ ي َّسَّعُّهُّمْ لَّز َّإذَّهُّمْ إ ِن  َّهُّمْ لَّمْ ي ُّو ْي َّوْإ مِن ْ   ق ِن َّلِ ق َّزِي ص َّه ِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ وَّ 
لَّكِن ْ إ ُّي ُّوإ مِن ْ مَّن ْغِ مَّن ْ مَّن َّعَّهُّمْ جَّق  َّهُّمْ لأَّ مِم َّا ق َّزَّض َّ  إَّلل َّهُّ لَّهُّمْ وَّ لَّوْ إ َّن  َّ إَّلن  َّاسَّ إ َّذ َّوْإ جُّق ُّوق َّهُّمْ لَّكَّان ُّوإ عَّان ِس ِي ن َّ ن ِح َّي ْزٍ
hadiths.en:
Musa ibn Bakr reported from Abu al -Hasan Imam Musa Al -Kadhim {a.s} who 
said: "Protect your wealth through Zakat."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 61 • Al-Muqni’ah, Part 1, p. 269 • 
Ghurar Al-Hikam, Part 1, p. 350 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 10, p. 43 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 9, p. 11 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, 
Vol. 9, p. 14 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 93, p. 13 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 7, p. 12 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 1, H. 1576

hadiths.chapter: 1. CHAPTER ON THE REASONS FOR THE OBLIGATION OF
hadiths.ar:
1578  -  وَّ ق َّالَّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ لِعَّم َّارِ ن ْن ِ مُّوسَّى إَّلس َّان َّاطِى ق َّالَّ ن َّعَّمْ ج ُّعِلْث ُّ  
ق ِذَّإك َّ ق َّال ح ُّ إَّلْحَّق  َّ إَّلْمَّعْلُّومَّ مِن ْ مَّالِك 
ق َّال ن َّى وَّ إَّلْن َّذَّن َّ ي َّن ْلَّى وَّ 
إَّلْعَّمَّلَّ ي َّن ْق َّى وَّ إَّلذ َّي  َّان َّ جَّى    لأَّ ي َّمُّوب ُّ ي َّا عَّم َّارُّ إ َّمَّا إ ِن  َّهُّ مَّا ق َّذ َّمْث َّ ق َّلَّن ْ ي َّسْن ِق َّك َّ وَّ مَّا إ َّج  َّزْب َّ ق َّلَّن ْ ي َّلْحَّق َّك
hadiths.en:
Hariz narrated from Zurara and Muhammad ibn Muslim that they both said to 
Abu Abdullah {a.s}:  
"What do you say about the verse of Allah {SWT} Almighty:   
‘Indeed, alms are only for the poor, the needy, those employed to collect them, those 
whose hearts are to be reconciled, for freeing captives, for those in debt, in the way of 
Allah {SWT}, and for the stranded traveller  -  an obligation imposed by Allah {SWT}’ (Surah 
At-Tawbah 9:60)? Should all these categories be given, even if they do not recognize 
(the Imam)?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "The Imam distributes to all of them because they acknowledge his 
authority."   
Zurara asked: "What if they do not recognize (the Imam)?"   
Imam {a.s} replied: "O Zurara, if he were to give to those who recognize and withhold 
from those who do not, there would be no recipients left for these funds. He gives to 
those who do not recognize in order to attract them to the religion and stabilize them 
in it. However, today, neither you nor your companions should give it except to those 
who recognize (the Imam). If you find among these Muslims someone who is aware, 
give it to him rather than others."  
Imam then added: "The share of ‘those whose hearts are to be reconciled’  and the 
share of ‘freeing captives’ is general, while the rest are specific."   
Zurara asked: "What if there are no eligible recipients?"   
Imam {a.s} replied: "A duty that Allah {SWT} Almighty has prescribed will never lack 
recipients."   
Zurara further asked: "What if the alms are not sufficient to meet their needs?"   
Imam responded: " Allah {SWT} Almighty has prescribed for the poor in the wealth of the 
rich what suffices them. Had He known that this would not suffice them, He would 
have increased it. The shortage is not due to Allah’s {SWT} decree, but due to those who 
withhold their due rights. Had people fulfilled their obligations, everyone would be 
living in ease."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 3, p. 496 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 4, p. 49 • 
Al-Wafi, Vol. 10, p. 163 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 9, p. 209 • Tafsir al-Burhan, Vol. 2, p. 796 • Tafsir Nur Al-
Thaqalayn, Part 2, p. 228 • Tafsir Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Part 5, p. 478 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 1, H. 1577

hadiths.chapter: 1. CHAPTER ON THE REASONS FOR THE OBLIGATION OF
hadiths.ar:
1579  -  وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ِ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ ج َّعْق َّزٍ إَّلْأ َّسَّذِى ِّ رَّص ِى َّ إَّلل َّهُّ عَّن ْهُّ عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ إ ِسْمَّاعِ ي لَّ إَّلْن َّزْمَّكِى ِّ عَّن ْ 
عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ِ إ َّجْمَّذَّ عَّن ِ إَّلْق َّص ْلِ ن ْن ِ إ ِسْمَّاعِي لَّ عَّن ْ مُّعَّي ِّث ٍ مَّوْلَّى إَّلص َّاذِق ِ عَّلَّ ي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ ق َّالَّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ 
« :اة َّ إ َّمْوَّإلِهِمْ مَّا ن َّق ِى َّ مُّسْلِم  ق َّق ِي زإً 
مُّحْي َّاج اً وَّ لأَّ سْي َّع ْن َّى ن ِمَّا ق َّزَّض َّ إَّلل َّهُّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ لَّهُّ وَّ إ ِن  َّ إَّلن  َّاسَّ مَّا إِق ْي َّق َّزُّوإ وَّ لأَّ إِجْي َّاج ُّوإ وَّ لأَّ ج َّاعُّوإ وَّ   لأَّ عَّزُّوإ إ ِلأ َّ 
ن ِذ ُّن ُّوب ِ إَّلْأ َّع ْن ِي َّاءِ وَّ جَّق ِي ق   عَّلَّى إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ إ َّن ْ ي َّمْن َّغَّ رَّجْمَّي َّهُّ مَّن ْ مَّن َّغَّ جَّق  َّ إَّ لل َّهِ ق ِى  مَّالِهِ وَّ إ ُّق ْسِمُّ ن ِال َّذ ِى  ج َّلَّق َّ إَّلْح َّلْق َّ 
وَّ ن َّسَّظَّ إَّلزِّر ْق َّ إ َّن  َّهُّ مَّا ص َّاعَّ مَّال  ق ِى  ن َّزٍّ وَّ لأَّ ن َّحْزٍ إ ِلأ َّ ن ِي َّزْك ِ إَّلز  َّكَّاة ِ وَّ مَّا صِي ذَّ صَّي ْذ  ق ِى  ن َّزٍّ  وَّ لأَّ ن َّحْزٍ إ ِلأ َّ ن ِي َّزْكِهِ إَّلي  َّسْن ِي حَّ 
ق ِى  ذ َّلِك َّ إَّلْي َّوْمِ وَّ   إ ِن  َّ إ َّجَّث  َّ إَّلن  َّاسِ إ ِلَّى إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ إ َّسْح َّاهُّمْ كَّق  اً وَّ إ َّسْح َّى إَّلن  َّاسِ مَّن ْ إ َّذ َّى ر َّكَّاة َّ مَّالِهِ وَّ  لَّمْ ي َّن ْح َّلْ 
عَّلَّى إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ ن ِمَّا إِق ْي َّزَّض َّ إَّلل َّهُّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ لَّهُّمْ ق ِى  مَّالِهِ
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said to Ammar ibn Musa al-Sabati:  
"O Ammar, you are a man of considerable wealth."   
Ammar replied: "Yes, may I be your ransom."  
Imam asked: "Do you fulfill what Allah {SWT} has made obligatory upon you in terms of 
Zakat?"  
Ammar said: "Yes."  
Imam then asked: "Do you give the known right from your wealth?"   
Ammar replied: "Yes."  
The Imam continued: "Do you maintain ties with your relatives?"   
Ammar said: "Yes."  
The Imam asked further: "Do you support your brothers?"   
Ammar replied: "Yes."  
The Imam then said: "O Ammar, wealth perishes, the body decays, and deeds remain. 
The Reckoner (Allah {SWT}) is living and does not die. O Ammar, indeed, what you have 
sent ahead will never leave you, and what you delay will never reach you."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 3, p. 501 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 27 • Al-Wafi, 
Vol. 10, p. 378 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 9, p. 50 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 1, H. 1578

hadiths.chapter: 1. CHAPTER ON THE REASONS FOR THE OBLIGATION OF
hadiths.ar:
1580  -  وَّ كَّي َّث َّ إَّلزِّص َّا عَّلِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ مُّوسَّى عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ ِلَّى مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ سِن َّان ٍ ق ِي مَّا كَّي َّث َّ إ ِلَّي ْهِ مِن ْ ج َّوَّإب ِ   مَّسَّان ِلِهِ 
عِل َّه َّ إَّلز  َّكَّاة ِ مِن ْ إ َّج ْلِ ق ُّوب ِ إَّلْق ُّق َّزَّإءِ وَّ ي َّحْصِي ن ِ إ َّمْوَّإلِ إَّلْأ َّع ْن ِي َّاءِ لِأ َّن  َّ إَّلل َّهَّ   عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ كَّل َّف َّ إ َّهْلَّ إَّلصِّح َّه ِ إَّلْق ِي َّامَّ ن ِس َّا ْن ِ إ َّهْلِ  
إَّلز  َّمَّان َّه ِ وَّ إَّلْن َّلْوَّى كَّمَّا ق َّالَّ إَّلل َّهُّ ي َّن َّارَّك َّ وَّ ي َّعَّالَّى، ۝  لَّي ُّن ْلَّوُّن  َّ ق ِى  إ َّمْوٰإلِكُّمْ وَّ إ َّن ْق ُّسِكُّمْ  ۝»
وَّ ق ِى  إ َّن ْق ُّسِكُّمْ  ي َّوْطِي ن ُّ إَّلْأ َّن ْق ُّسِ عَّلَّى إَّلص َّن ْزِ مَّغَّ مَّا ق ِى  ذ َّلِك َّ مِن ْ إ َّذَّإءِ س ُّكْزِ ن ِعَّمِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ وَّ إَّلط َّمَّغِ  ق ِى  إَّلز ِّي َّاذَّة ِ 
مَّغَّ مَّا ق ِي هِ مِن َّ إَّلز ِّي َّاذَّة ِ وَّ إَّلز َّإ ْق َّه ِ وَّ إَّلز َّجْمَّه ِ لِأ َّهْلِ إَّلص  َّعْف ِ وَّ إَّلْعَّطْف ِ عَّلَّى إ َّهْلِ  إَّلْمَّسْكَّن َّه ِ وَّ إَّلْحَّث ِّ لَّهُّمْ عَّلَّى إَّلْمُّوَّإسَّاة ِ 
وَّ ي َّق ْوِي َّه ِ إَّلْق ُّق َّزَّإءِ وَّ إَّلْمَّعُّون َّه ِ لَّهُّمْ عَّلَّى إ َّمْزِ إَّلذِّي ن ِ وَّ هُّوَّ عِط َّه   لِأ َّهْلِ إَّلْع ِن َّى وَّ عِن ْزَّة   لَّهُّ مْ لِي َّسْي َّذِل ُّوإ عَّلَّى ق ُّق َّزَّإءِ إَّلْأ ج ِزَّة ِ  
ن ِهِمْ وَّ مَّا لَّهُّمْ مِن َّ  إَّلْحَّث ِّ ق ِى  ذ َّلِك َّ عَّلَّى إَّلس  ُّكْزِ لِل َّهِ ي َّن َّارَّك َّ وَّ ي َّعَّالَّى لِمَّا ج َّو َّلَّهُّمْ وَّ إ َّعْطَّاهُّمْ وَّ إَّلذ ُّعَّاءِ وَّ إَّلي  َّ ص َّز ُّعِ وَّ إَّلْح َّوْف ِ 
مِن ْ إ َّن ْ ي َّصِي زُّوإ مِي ْلَّهُّمْ ق ِى  إ ُّمُّورٍ كَّي ِي زَّة ٍ ق ِى  إ َّذَّإءِ إَّلز  َّكَّاة ِ وَّ إَّلص َّذَّق َّاب ِ وَّ صِلَّه ِ إَّلْأ َّ رْجَّامِ وَّ إِصْطِن َّاعِ إَّلْمَّعْزُّوف ِ
hadiths.en:
In a narration from Abu al -Husayn Muhammad ibn Ja'far al -Asadi, may Allah 
{SWT} be pleased with him, from Muhammad ibn Isma'il al -Barmaki, from Abdullah ibn 
Ahmad, from al -Fadl ibn Isma'il, from Mu'attib, the servant of Imam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}:  
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Zakat was ordained as a test for the wealthy and as support for the poor. If people 
paid the Zakat on their wealth, no Muslim would remain poor or in need. They would 
find sufficiency through what Allah {SWT}, the Almighty and Majestic, has prescribed for 
them. People only become impoverished, needy, hungry, or unclothed because of the 
sins of the wealthy. It is a truth upon Allah {SWT}, the Almighty and Majestic, that He 
withholds His mercy from those who withhold Allah’s {SWT} right in their wealth.  
I swear by the One who created creation and spread sustenance that no wealth is lost 
on land or sea except due to the neglect of Zakat. And no prey is hunted on land or sea 
except due to the neglect of glorification (of Allah {SWT}) on that day.  
Indeed, the most beloved of people to Allah {SWT}, the Almighty and Majestic, are the 
most generous in giving, and the most generous of people is the one who pays the 
Zakat of his wealth and does not withhold from the believers what Allah {SWT}, the 
Almighty and Majestic, has ordained for them in his wealth."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 1, p. 370 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 10, p. 50 
• Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 9, p. 12 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 1, H. 1579

hadiths.chapter: 1. CHAPTER ON THE REASONS FOR THE OBLIGATION OF
hadiths.ar:
1581 - وَّ ق َّالَّ إ َّن ُّو إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ مُّوسَّى ن ْن ُّ ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّم ق ِى  مَّوْص ِعِهَّا لَّمْ 
ي ُّسْا َّلْ مِن ْ إ َّي ْن َّ إِكْي َّسَّث َّ مَّالَّه
hadiths.en:
Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha {a.s} wrote to Muhammad ibn Sinan in response to 
his questions:  
"The reason for the obligation of Zakat is to provide sustenance for the poor and to 
protect the wealth of the rich. Allah {SWT}, the Almighty and Majestic, has tasked the 
healthy with caring for those who are afflicted and tested, as Allah {SWT}, Blessed and 
Exalted, says: 'You will surely be tested in your wealth and your souls' (Surah Ali ’Imran 
3:186).  
In your wealth, it refers to the giving of Zakat, and in your souls, it refers to preparing 
yourselves for patience. This also serves as an expression of gratitude for Allah’s {SWT} 
blessings, an aspiration for an increase in provision, and a means of fostering 
compassion and mercy for the weak, sympathy for the impoverished, and 
encouragement for mutual assistance and solidarity.   
It strengthens the poor and aids them in their religious duties. It also serves as a 
reminder and a lesson for the wealthy to reflect on the poor of the Hereafter through 
the poor of this world. It inspires them to thank Allah {SWT}, Blessed and Exalted, for what 
He has bestowed upon them, and to make supplications, humble themselves, and 
fear becoming like the impoverished in many matters.   
These include fulfilling Zakat, giving in charity, maintaining ties of kinship, and 
performing acts of kindness."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Ilal al-Sharai’, Vol. 2, p. 369 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 10, p. 50 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 9, p. 12 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 93, p. 18 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 1, p. 74 • Tafsir 
Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Part 1, p. 403 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 1, H. 1580

hadiths.chapter: 1. CHAPTER ON THE REASONS FOR THE OBLIGATION OF
hadiths.ar:
1582  -  وَّ ق َّالَّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم   عِس ْزِي ن َّ ذِرْهَّماً لِأ َّن  َّهُّ 
عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ج َّلَّق َّ إَّلْح َّلْق َّ ق َّعَّلِمَّ ع َّن ِي  َّهُّمْ وَّ ق َّق ِي زَّهُّمْ وَّ ق َّوِي  َّهُّمْ وَّ ص َّعِي ق َّهُّمْ  ق َّح َّعَّلَّ مِن ْ كُّلِّ إ َّلْف ٍ ج َّمْسَّه ً وَّ عِس ْزِي ن َّ مِسْكِي ن اً  
وَّ لَّوْ لأَّ ذ َّلِك َّ لَّز َّإذَّهُّمُّ إَّلل َّهُّ لِأ َّن  َّهُّ ج َّالِق ُّهُّمْ وَّ هُّوَّ إ َّعْلَّمُّ ن ِهِمْ.
hadiths.en:
Abu al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim {a.s} said:  
"Whoever pays the full Zakat on his wealth and allocates it properly will not be 
questioned about how he earned his wealth."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 3, p. 504 • Thawab al-A’mal wa Iqab al-
A’mal, Part 1, p. 46 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 10, p. 44 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 9, p. 218 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 93, p. 19 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 1, H. 1581
